title: File name not resolving in Write File node
symptom: When attempting to download a file, the file name is set to the binary variable name instead of the actual file name. This results in a file named '{{ $binary.data.fileName }}' instead of the expected name.
applies_when: File name is derived from binary data
root_cause: On Windows, the path format can affect how n8n resolves the file name from binary data. Using backslashes in the file path can lead to issues with variable resolution, causing the binary variable name to be used instead of the actual file name.
fix_steps: Change the file path in the Write File node to use forward slashes instead of backslashes.
Save the workflow and try downloading the file again.
agent_instruction: Update the file path in the Write File node to use forward slashes.
